patches.find: Eschatological prophecy mapped to dome geometry axis. No measurable consequence exists in the present; no falsification criteria are specified; the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author. The dome's own monitoring system excludes WIN-032, and the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics. Counting future supernatural events as confirmed predictions inflates the model's scorecard. Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test — though for a different reason than the dome’s numerical WINs. This is not a derived quantity that can be checked against measurement; it is a categorical assertion (theological, symbolic, or definitional) with no observable to confirm or refute. Because no empirical measurement could distinguish a world where this claim is true from one where it is false, the distinction between prediction and retrodiction is moot — there was never a testable proposition to pre-register. Under the dome author’s own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, counting unfalsifiable assertions alongside empirically measured quantities inflates the model’s scorecard.
patches.replace: Eschatological prophecy mapped to dome geometry axis. No measurable consequence exists in the present; no falsification criteria are specified; the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21) but was added by the dome author. The dome's own monitoring system excludes WIN-032, and the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics. Counting future supernatural events as confirmed predictions inflates the model's scorecard. A dome advocate might reply that this critique applies the unfalsifiability test asymmetrically — demanding measurable consequences for WIN-032 while exempting mainstream claims about pre-CMB cosmology, black-hole interiors, or string-theoretic vacua, all of which are currently untestable. That objection has a kernel of truth: standard physics does contain currently-untestable extrapolations, and a consistent methodology has to address them. But the comparison fails where it matters. Physicists do not label the black-hole interior “CONFIRMED” and tally it in the same scorecard as the measured cosmic microwave background temperature; they classify it as a theoretical consequence of General Relativity whose status derives from the exterior tests that have been verified, and they report it separately. WIN-032 occupies the inverse position: an assertion with zero testable consequence carries the CONFIRMED tag and sits in the same accuracy ratio as Schumann resonances and magnetic-field anomalies that have explicit numerical tolerances. The dome author is free to define CONFIRMED as “consistent with the model's theological wrapper” — but that definition cannot coexist in the same scorecard with measurements that carry falsification thresholds. Either CONFIRMED has one meaning across all WINs, in which case WIN-032 does not qualify, or the dome must publish separate scorecards (empirical vs. theological). Mixing the two is the category error; the fix is not to relitigate the theology but to insist on a single definition of confirmation — which is what our Scorecard-Inflation argument (Part 3.1b, Pattern 6) already does. Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test — though for a different reason than the dome's numerical WINs. This is not a derived quantity that can be checked against measurement; it is a categorical assertion (theological, symbolic, or definitional) with no observable to confirm or refute. Because no empirical measurement could distinguish a world where this claim is true from one where it is false, the distinction between prediction and retrodiction is moot — there was never a testable proposition to pre-register. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, counting unfalsifiable assertions alongside empirically measured quantities inflates the model's scorecard.
patches.rationale: EXP-174: Insert preemptive paragraph neutralizing asymmetric-unfalsifiability defense (curmudgeon cycle-3, defense_survives=3/5). Addresses advocate argument that review applies unfalsifiability test asymmetrically vs standard physics. Shows comparison fails: mainstream physics separates theoretical consequences from empirical confirmations, does not tally them in same scorecard.
